commands.review_summary:
The PR changes managed npm plugin installs to use generation-specific project roots, marks replaced npm package trees as retained, and skips retained generations during recovery and doctor cleanup.

PR surface: Source +495, Tests +956. Total +1451 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review: current main can remove the previous managed npm install directory after recording a new install, and the linked Windows logs show a live gateway importing a removed @openclaw/codex chunk. I did not establish a fresh live current-main reproduction in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are live upgrade proof and a maintainer retention-policy decision, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix: npm plugin updates break running gateway imports
target.body:
Related: #85844

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users updating managed npm/ClawHub plugins could keep a running gateway pointed at old hashed `dist` chunks while the updater removed that old package tree. In the observed Windows 2026.6.10-beta.1 failure, the gateway later failed to talk to the user because Node could not import a stale `run-attempt-*.js` file from the previous `@openclaw/codex` install.

This PR addresses the managed npm plugin replacement slice of #85844. It does not try to solve every stale core-dist or process-restart lifecycle case.

## Why This Change Was Made

Managed npm updates now install into artifact-generation project roots instead of replacing the previous package directory in place. Replaced npm generations stay on disk for live imports, but OpenClaw records an OpenClaw-owned retained marker during the install-record commit so registry recovery and doctor repair do not treat them as active installs.

The marker state is kept in the same commit/rollback path as install records: old generations are marked only after the next records are written, active retained generations are unmarked when committed, and marker changes are restored if the config commit rolls back. Install/update policy mode remains tied to the user-visible operation, so retained-only generations do not turn fresh installs into policy updates.

## User Impact

Users can update an npm-backed plugin without breaking already-running code that still imports chunks from the previous plugin generation. After restart or recovery, OpenClaw sees only the committed active generation; retained old generations are ignored by registry recovery and protected from destructive doctor cleanup.

Operators still get install-policy semantics: retained-only package trees do not make a fresh `install` or forced update look like a real update.

## Evidence

Real before evidence: #85844 includes a Windows 2026.6.10-beta.1 gateway failure where `@openclaw/codex` tried to import a missing stale hashed dist chunk after update: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85844#issuecomment-4761855575

Focused after-patch validation:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts` (52 passed, 3 skipped)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.test.ts` (106 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.test.ts` (13 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-install-persist.test.ts` (15 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts` (21 passed, 1 skipped)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts` (11 passed)

Static validation:

- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.test.ts src/cli/plugins-install-persist.ts src/cli/plugins-install-persist.test.ts src/plugins/install-paths.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-record-reader.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/managed-npm-retention.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.test.ts src/cli/plugins-install-persist.ts src/cli/plugins-install-persist.test.ts src/plugins/install-paths.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-record-reader.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/managed-npm-retention.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`

Review closeout:

- `$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'; python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`
- Result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

Not rerun in this branch: a full live Windows gateway upgrade. Earlier local npm-spec E2E was blocked before reaching this code path by `spawnSync npm ENOENT`, so this PR relies on focused installer/commit/recovery/doctor tests plus the real issue log for before evidence.
